content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


November 12, 1985




Sherry Passmore
Executive Director
Citizens Property Rights Committee
P.O. Box 1332
Temple City, CA  91780

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice;
                                             Our File No. A-85-233

Dear Ms. Passmore:

     Thank you for your letter dated November 6, 1985,
requesting advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).
However, I cannot advise you about someone else's duties under
the Political Reform Act.  See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239
(copy enclosed).  Whether Commissioner Montgomery can
participate in consideration of Commission regulations on
redevelopment decisions is discussed in general terms in my
letter to him dated November 1, 1985.  I believe you already
have a copy of that letter.  I am unable to give you any
additional advice concerning Commissioner Montgomery's duties
under the Political Reform Act.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel

BAM:nwm
Enclosure




428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Nov 12 8 24 AH '05

Citizens Property Rights Committee


November 6, 1985


Ms. Barbara A. Milman, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission, Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Milman,

I am hereby requesting written advice, under government Code Section
813114 (b) with respect to the following:

Would a commissioner of the Fair Political Practices Commission be in
conflict of interest under provisions of the Political Reform Act if he
is participating in discussions, drafting of or voting on any regulations
or decisions affecting redevelopment agencies at the same time that he is
deriving income as redevelopment counsel to a number of redevelopment
agencies?

Thank you for your attention to this request.

Sincerely,

[illegible]
Sherry Passmore
Executive Director, Citizens Property Rights Committee

SP/lcr

cc. Chairman Stanford, Commissioners Lee, Lemons, Montgomery, and Roden.


P.O. Box 1332 • Temple City, California 91780 • (818) 405-1091 • (818) 447-5500

sections.question: Would a commissioner of the Fair Political Practices Commission be in conflict of interest under provisions of the Political Reform Act if he is participating in discussions, drafting of or voting on any regulations or decisions affecting redevelopment agencies at the same time that he is deriving income as redevelopment counsel to a number of redevelopment agencies?
sections.conclusion: I cannot advise you about someone else's duties under the Political Reform Act. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 (copy enclosed). Whether Commissioner Montgomery can participate in consideration of Commission regulations on redevelopment decisions is discussed in general terms in my letter to him dated November 1, 1985.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether a FPPC Commissioner would have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act if he participates in FPPC discussions, drafting, or voting on regulations affecting redevelopment agencies while simultaneously receiving income as counsel to redevelopment agencies.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC General Counsel declined to provide advice about another person's (Commissioner Montgomery's) duties under the Political Reform Act, citing regulatory restrictions on providing such advice. The FPPC had previously addressed the conflict of interest question directly to Commissioner Montgomery in a separate letter.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (dated November 6, 1985) and the FPPC's response (dated November 12, 1985). The FPPC declined to provide the requested advice based on 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239, which restricts advice about others' duties. File No. A-85-233. Addressee name error in salutation ('Dear Ms. Passmore' when responding to Sherry Passmore's request, though Sherry Passmore is listed as Executive Director in the letterhead).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would a commissioner of the Fair Political Practices Commission be in conflict of interest under provisions of the Political Reform Act if he is participating in discussions, drafting of or voting on any regulations or decisions affecting redevelopment agencies at the same time that he is deriving income as redevelopment counsel to a number of redevelopment agencies?

CONCLUSION: I cannot advise you about someone else's duties under the Political Reform Act. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239 (copy enclosed). Whether Commissioner Montgomery can participate in consideration of Commission regulations on redevelopment decisions is discussed in general terms in my letter to him dated November 1, 1985.

embedding.summary: This document consists of an incoming request from Citizens Property Rights Committee asking whether a FPPC Commissioner has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act for participating in redevelopment regulations while earning income from redevelopment agencies, and the FPPC's declination to advise on another person's duties.
